reviews.review_msg: Dr Punja's class was amazing and very engaging. His lectures (and slides) are really informative and I 100% recommend going to them. The midterm and final, I believe were fair and really tested our knowledge of the materials covered. I recommend deeply understanding the material and knowing even the littlest things as they might be important.

Great professor. He is sooo funny, and his lectures are actually fun to attend. He also provides questions for his lecture slides, which help figure out what is important and what is not. Doesn't require you to buy the textbook and provides everything in his lecture slides, which is amazing. Made the lecture component of bisc101 super easy.
BEST PROF EVER!!!!!!

Amazing professor!!! Always makes lecture fun to engage and explains complex subject in a simple way to understand. Definitely a heavy-reading course, but Dr. Punja makes it so much easier and fun. He also gives nice feedbacks when I ask questions. He made me enjoy biology so much , will absolutely take his class again.

Fantastic prof! Entertaining in class and actively tries to keep thing interesting and engaging. Very passionate about his work w plants and covers material inan easy to follow along way. Literally sang songs, rapped, and read out his own poems in class, if you get the chance to take a class with him do it!
he makes the lectures very easy to follow along and explains every topic properly, only downside is the midterm exam was graded horribly but i blame peter
Very fun lectures.

Zamir is a fun and passionate prof who makes biology engaging with his raps and jokes. While the exams focus on memorization, using resources like Unstuckstudy alongside the textbook can help you ace this class.
Awesome teacher who is clearly passionate. Really funny and generous, overall my favourite teacher I've had here. Definitely memorization heavy for tests but they're still fair.
was really nice

He is a great prof! He explains the material in a clear and concise manner, and never fails to make and us laugh with his poems and rap. He tests fair, but memorize all the content that is in his slides as his exams are more memorization than applications of specific processes. Overall if you have to take any biology class, take one with him!

A really passionate professor who seems to really care about his students. The lecture are entertaining and you get useful practice questions every week. His exams though aren't great. They contain questions that are unrelated to the field of bio and are rather for example what he said is a good study tip. He looks for very specific keywords to.
Very good prof. Attend lectures do his practice and you will be fine. Great lectures and prioritizes understanding
Super fun and passionate professor. He makes an obvious effort to engage students and has colourful engaging lecture slides. Definitely get ready to memorize for the tests!
Great rapper!
funny guy, boring lectures but he did a rap for us at the end of the sem

His slides are full of pictures and very colourful. They are some of the best slides to look at. He also had some entertainment going on at the end of the semester to wish me good luck on my exam. Arf-arf.

As a bisc major - hand's down best bio prof at SFU. Yeah the lecture videos are long as hell but the flipped classroom method was the best kind of practice and motivation. He attended ALL Labs to see his students and stopped by all the desks to talk to us. Really funny and great at explaining ; also dense but helpful slides!

I heard about this course being tough but it is safe to say I was lucky to have Dr. Bains and Dr. Punja.  Dr. B taught most of the lectures and I found the flipped classroom method a huge help.  His practice questions during lecture were similar to the final exam.  Without this, I don't think I would have ended up with the grade I did.

Practice questions during lecture were super useful!  Student learning objectives helped big time for the midterm and final.  Also, enjoyed Dr. B.'s stories related to different human organ systems - especially the ones he had to deal with related to the circulatory system.  Finally, he is super approachable - always willing to answer questions.

Flipped classroom in lecture worked for me.  All the practice questions we went over helped big time for the short answer section of the final exam.  During the midterm and final exams, Dr. Bains came to the CAL office regularly to address any questions that I had.  He replied to my questions via email right away.  Thank you for being awesome.

I love having both Dr. Bains and Dr. Punja as Instructors this term.  Both have different teaching techniques, but my friends and I are enjoying them.  Dr. Bains is clearly passionate about biology and he provides plenty of practice questions for the midterm and final exam.  The online videos are informative (but may be a bit long at times).
Give misleading feedback. Not helpful at all.

Each lecture has an hour+ long pre-lecture that covers the material. All of the actual lecture is just working through practice questions. Pre-lecs are interesting, actual lecs are not. Lots of material on the exams. Claims he will only curve the final grades in our favour.

Class Average for final was a 49%, if you were wondering if you should take this class take it with someone else. He has a bunch of unnecessary tasks throughout the course like a forest diary and a forest walk uncorrelated with the course content. Ron was extremely rude when I saw him in office hours despite nagging us to come for help. AVOID !!!

Dude is the most unprofessional prof I've ever met, not only do his lecture slides not have words on it... he posts the most random questions completely unrelated to biology in our exams... How is anyone even supposed to know that. Overall he was a lazy guy who should just retire now instead of suffocating more students.

Final exam and lab finals were very difficult. Tutorials definitely are a lifesaver if you want to do well in the course. Lectures are not the most interesting but are crucial to the midterm and final exam. She is super caring but do not let that deceive you into thinking her tests are easy!

She's really easy to talk to and she provides a lot of materials. Textbook is unnecessary, her handouts are pretty informative and any personal notes as well. Be very specific in your wording on exams because she looks for keywords in order to provide a mark. The lab exam is chaotic, make sure you prepare well for it.

She was an awesome professor to have as a first year student. The exams are fair and decent if you know your material. Textbook isn't required, just make sure to pay attention in lectures and go through lecture slides in your own time. I recommend making your own notes for lectures. The lab final exam was very hard so start studying weeks prior.

she isn't that bad you just have to be accountable of your own learning. lecture slides aren't the best, packed with info that is unnecessary at times. midterm and exam is hella easy if you know the material. really kind and caring person though

This class was way too hard as the lectures slides were confusing and the labs were useless for us to get prepared for the exams. Overall, a tough grader and the tests don't make sense. I would recommend not taking her!!!!!!

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. I did not listen to people when they said don't take it but I wish I listened. Her test were not related to the material and VERY DIFFICULT. Each lecture had 70+ slides. She gives test study guides but the tests are the exact opposite. Lab final was difficult as well. Try as hard as you want but that A will never be seen.

ivona was really engaging. there were in class quizzes which made attendance mandatory. also weekly at home quizzes that ‘forced' you to review the material each week. TAs were really helpful, please talk to them if u need (ivona was also extremely approachable). labs weren't mandatory attendance but i recommend going to strength ur understanding.
For all first years I would say avoid this prof and instead of taking this class at the Surrey Campus do yourselves a favour and take it in Burnaby.

Very friendly and approachable to questions/feedback. Lectures are content heavy in the span of 50min. but if you review and catch up its not too difficult. She also has handouts that sum up her lectures, self tests after each unit for practice and the textbook for extra help. Tests are fair and the lab exam is easy if you do the work

She only taught a part of Bisc 101, so I don't know how she is in courses where she is the only instructor. But, as far as this course went, she was a good professor. Her lectures were very engaging, and she seemed to care about the learning of the students. Overall helpful and open to questions.

She is an amazing person no doubt, but her lectures are not very helpful if you don't do the pre-class work. Midterm 1 was tough, study from the lecture slide questions as well as DO ALL PRE-READINGS!

Nice person but needs some more practice teaching this course. She clearly knows what she is talking about but she is clearly used to teaching people who already know all the basics so as an intro class it's hard to keep up.

Megan Barker is kind, but covers hardly anything during lectures.Tests are basically just stuff she expects you to read from the textbook, and you're expected to read 30-60 pages a week. Lecture attendance is mandatory, but she covers nothing in the lectures, just lots of iClicker. My TA, Haninder, was the only reason I actually got an A.

Dr. Barker approaches this course with a focus in extending our learning and majority of test questions apply concepts to new examples, which is valuable. However, becomes challenging when key textbook concepts that we are tested on are not thoroughly reviewed in class. While she is very kind, the way course material is tested needs improvement.

She would assign 15-30 page textbook reading each week and you would need to take notes on all of it. In lectures she taught almost nothing and only went over the brief important stuff. Get ready to learn everything on your own.

She is a mediocre teacher. Very unclear about what the wants to teach and waste of time. Spent a lot of time going on side tangents about other things. She is not good at teaching as you learn more from textbook than her. You spend hours on the reading to have proper reading comp.

she is a good person but her lectures are very unhelpful . Nothing from lectures is on exams and all you will learn is from the pre readings which takes all your time. The course journals has no point, very time consuming. Try to avoid if possible. 
There are so many things you need to do each week, so don't fall behind.

Dr. Megan is a nice person but doesn't teach any content and tends to waste time of lectures through discussing and very disorganized. It's a textbook focused course as it's the only way to learn. Both midterms she didn't give the students time and cut the final time in half. The lab exam had 8 stations for 5 mins each which made many not pass.

Every lecture wasn't worth the commute. I had to hope for the best with every test, and thank goodness it came through. Can't lie, the labs were decent. The group project is a huge NO. For lab, create a group with people that can actually COMMUNICATE. Megan's slides consist of iClicker questions and a bit of information. Don't rely on the slides.

In my opinion, Megan Barker is mediocre. As a person, she is welcoming and nice, but when you go up to her to ask questions, she's confused. Her lectures were mostly useless, and she forces iClicker questions, making it mandatory to attend as they are a part of your grade. Course-wise, love bio but I learned more from the textbook than Megan. MID
My worst experience couldn't tolerate her. She should actually see these feedbacks and maybe work in future.

What for the reason to give us only 1:30 hr to do the final exam when the exam schedule literally said 3 hrs. Thats so so unfair what does she think we are some kind of machine to think and do 60question with several parts in 1:30hr.

She is bad at her job no matter how sweet she tries to be but can never teach. We pay fee to be taught not to have a sweet person to talk. She didn't teach us a word the whole semester like 1% from 100%

I don't think that Professor Megan was bad so much because the half questions from bubble questions were taken from the canvas online quizzes or iclicker. You should do all online canvas quizzes and her mock tests for every midterm and especially finals because it'll help you. The main problem is 90 minutes for exam that was unfair!
If the schedule says we get 3 hours to write a final exam, then we should get the full 3 hours. Not 90 minutes.
Her dog could teach better.

DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE. Megan seemed more confused than me throughout the semester. She doesn't teach anything while assigning over 30 pages of pre reading. The whole lecture consist on her getting us to “discuss” with the people next to us.  Don't even get me started on her course journal and feedback atp she really needs to take that feedback.

First off, why was the exam time halved/shortened drastically?? It was stated that we would have 3 full hours to work on the exam but we were actually given 1 hour and 30 mins to work on the actual exam while another 30 mins for the group component. This is so unacceptable and frustrating. If an exam says it's 3 hours, I'd EXPECT it to be 3 hours.
Save yourself and never do this course with her. As she will never teach anything about what to expect on the test and you are on you own.

she is the worst prof i have ever had in my entire life. she makes us do weekly readings from the textbook and makes us read like a gazillion pages and we never know to what extent we need to learn the info. then, during lectures, she does not teach anything she just makes you talk to people around u, then she makes us do course journals every week
Good professor engages with students very well

If we get 3 hrs to write the final exam then give us 3 full hours why only giving 2 hours to complete 60 questions. It doesn't even made sense and plus it was so hard something we never learned in class.
Don't do it

genuinely hate her with all of my heart. her slides look like they were pulled from a kids book + have nothing of note on them. stupid fking preclass quizzes/journals every fking week + those GODDAMN iclickers. in love with "talk to the person beside you!!" stfu
tests do not correlate to taught material AT ALL. she just yaps n yaps abt random sht.

This course has been very underwhelming and very ridiculous. I have no clue as to why she made us throw paper across the lecture hall during exam review. Why are you making the custodians' job even harder than it is? She expects everyone to know the textbook in and out like its ABCs and assumes everyone had a PhD in biology. Save your sanity.

She does not know how to teach; her slides are very shallow, and you can't really study or learn from them. You have to do so much reading every single week, and all she does in class is test your pre-reading knowledge.
Do yourself a favour never take her. Ridiculous i payed $4000 for this only course as an international and she is so bad at her job. I wish i could have my money back

So unrealistic that I am paying $500+ for a course only to end up self studying the entire textbook. Other classes actively have 20-50 slides that ACTUALLY contain material that is on assessments and are also material heavy, so there is no reason why Megan cannot do the same. There needs to be a lot of reflecting for better teaching and learning.
I was praying to Jesus she was a good professor, she made me lose my faith in the lord.
Yall know that scene in mean girls when Regina is writing in the burn book? Yeah, that all I have to say about this professor.

do NOT take this class with her. shows "cute animals" during the horrid tests she gives us to "relieve stress" as if thats gonna change the monstrosity of the test she gave that has nothing we learnt in class. lecture slides are all pictures and expects us magically know everything from the hundreds of textbook readings she assigns like be so fr.

i'm paying like 4k a year to teach myself everything in this class. megan absolutely cannot teach, her time management is horrible and she brings her dog to classes after people tell her theyre allergic and it barks and she pretends to be annoyed. my ta teaches me more in one hour tutorials than her three hour ones and she gets paid way more. js no

I honestly don't know how she is a professor. Her lectures are so useless and you have to teach yourself everything. The lecture slides are bio12 level and the test is far more complicated than you can imagine. Not to mention there are so many pre-class work you have to do before each lecture including a very useless course journal.

A disgrace to all professors, how are you here?  She teaches absolutely nothing and expects you to know the entire textbook. Her lectures are useless, and her dog barks the entire class. If you thought you were good at bio before, think again, cause you're not. Even if you study all day, all night, you will barely pass. Just pray you get a good TA.

worst professor ever, doesn't know what she's talking about. too much group work, cant teach for the life of her, her dogs annoying asf big distraction, thinks it'll help us cus "cuddling with animals helps with stress bs", like YOU and YOUR DOG CAUSE the stress? dont ever take a class with her. I could care less if she's a nice person, she sucks

Do not get me wrong, she's a kind person.
Except, there is infinite pre-readings, weekly journals, lectures really don't help with the textbook and exam material. If you want to take this course, prepare to become independent, because you will be teaching yourself everything.

Lectures are useless, you basically teach yourself everything, she's is always behind. I literally don't know how she is a prof. I'm not denying that she is a great person, but that doesn't make up for how bad she is at her job. I just want to be done with this class already.

Please avoid her class. Worst professor ever. Too many course journals which are completely useless. The test concepts are different than lectures and it takes forever to get through all the pre-class readings.
It's so bad I'm actually just sitting here laughing. Like how is she getting paid for this LMAO

the tests are completely new things never seen before. Her lectures consists of her telling us to talk to our peers about what we think the answer might be. her slides are all pictures and no actual notes. she is not teaching you anything and has weekly journals which are so time consuming and not even helpful with the class itself. avoid class!!!!

If you're not religious, well you better start pick one and start praying. Megan's lecture slides are inefficient enough to cover ANY study material for midterms and exams; she expects students to know the entire textbook by heart. Yeah she's friendly and kind but that's not the point, we're paying for this education so I'd expect a good education.

Professor Barker is an incredibly passionate and kind professor. She cares about engaging the class during lectures, is always open to feedback, and is super accessible for questions. There is quite a bit of reading, but her lectures focus on applying that reading in practice questions, which works for some, but maybe not others.
Very passionate prof, makes class engaging and fun. Work load is fairly large, but the material necessitates that. Would highly recommend.
Get ready to read a LOT, and prepare to teach yourself everything.

Megan seems like a wonderful and passionate professor,but she doesn't structure her lectures well. The only reason I went to the lecture was because of participation marks(which are pointless if you're confused about half the material).She does way too many "discuss with the person beside you,"and it leaves no time for a topic overview.Not worth it

Spends the first half of class yapping about things that no one cares about and only brushes over the important stuff in the last 20 minutes.
An overload of pre-class/pre-lab quizzes every week, + the pointless weekly journal that doesn't help you in any way.
Prepare to teach yourself EVERYTHING. Absolutely useless professor. Avoid at all costs.

do NOT take her if you value your GPA. I love bio, studied a lot, thought I understood the material, only to have nothing we talked about in lecture on the exams. everything she structured was very pointless in my opinion.

Megan is nothing more than an undergrad herself if it wasn't for the textbook—which carries the course more than she ever could. Every week she sends out a two page document that lists the ten thousand chapters that need to be read prior to class, all so that she could make slides with zero information and assign two quizzes + one journal weekly .

I have never put so much time into a class and done so poorly. This class made me rethink my whole life. Would not take if you care about your GPA. Claims tests are fair but it's a self taught course and tests are unpredictable. ALOT of unnecessary quizzes,lab prep,journals. Not worth your happiness. Run while you can, not everyone has that luxury.

One of the worst profs I've had at SFU. Constant “talk to the person beside you” during class instead of actually teaching the material. I have taught all of this course to myself. Her class averages for exams say more about her teaching than student performance- test material is nothing like the lectures. Avoid if you care about ur GPA.

She is the worst prof ever. Decently nice but that doesn't when your grades and gpa are on the line. You can study your hardest, use every technique and still get below average. Averages are low 60s every test with only 5 people receiving 90-100.
Please avoid her class if you care about your GPA.

Avoid her at any cost, worst professor i ever had. The lectures are useless you will have no idea what to expect on exams they are out of no where and so hard. Also, the tutorials are bullshit TA doesn't even know what and how to teach and have no idea what is going on in the course. She should stop teaching and her dog should stay at home.
Materials on test is nothing we learned in lectures
Avoid if possible.

She's actually so nice, but she's one of the hardest profs I've had in a long time. I don't understand what gets done from group discussions, weekly journals, or just not teaching. Exams are hard as hell, we're not phD students, so why are we being tested on things that are irrelevant? Idk avoid if you can, nice lady, but will screw your gpa over
she's about to cram a whole month of material into one week bc she wasted too much time making us talk to our partners. nice person but horrible at time management. avoid if you can.

this class is just the worst. majority of what is on the test is not covered in class. if it is, she goes over it for 5 minutes. the rest of the class consists of "talking to your neighbours". bro. avoid if possible. she sucks and lowkey I think she's racist.

Class is a waste of time, the questions she puts on the written portions of her exams are always so extra compared to the material taught (sometimes stuff we have never even talked about). The questions always feel like a trick because they could all be right but which one is the “most” right…

Dr. Barker is a great person, but not a great professor. In simple terms, she can't teach. Her lectures and weekly feedback journals are a waste of time, the pre-lab work is time-consuming and most of your studying will be out of class. The ppts are nearly impossible to study off, and the labs don't prepare you enough for the exam.

Taking her class this semster. She's a very energetic teacher but her lectures are almost useless when it comes to the second midterm. The lecture material was in the midterm, yes but her  questions were very confusing, she also crammed this semester and barely went through much of the material that was in the second midterm. Overall not that good.

Her lectures are pointless and a waste of time, I don't know where she pulls half the question on her exams from. Assigns way too much reading from textbook and makes it really unclear how in depth we need to learn things. Overcomplicates the entire course, I recommend avoiding a class with her.
Instead of making her students do weekly feedback journals, she should just take a look at her rating here.

Her lecture is completely useless. Every week we are wasting time sitting in her lecture to discuss question which the content she didn't teach us yet. She just made you read the entire textbook by yourself and basically study by yourself. However she loves to hear feedback from students by making us write this pointless weekly journal.

Currently taking her class — she's very kind and knowledgeable, but not the easiest to learn from. Her slides can be unclear, so you'll need to teach yourself most of the material. The reading guides help, but the tests are tricky and require a strong grasp of the content to do well. Overall, she's a nice person but not my favorite professor.
She does not know how to teach. She should go back to teaching only the lab portion of the course.

She likes to think of herself as being relatable and kind to students, but if she really did understand students; she would not assign so much work. You have to do these reading guides every week and teach yourself, lectures are very unhelpful and boring. I don't know how she got all these high ratings.

She's always late, and there is so much pre-reading to do. Almost every lecture, she is a pre-reading week behind, or she doesn't even teach what is on the pre-reading. We have these weekly journals that don't even benefit you. She's nice and makes us engage with others, but being nice doesn't make you a good professor.

Class time was wasted by discussing clicker questions with the person beside us. The lectures don't cover much info, she assigns weekly readings and you have to self teach yourself. sample test questions were kind of helpful.

She claims her tests are fair but it's not, you need to do 60 questions (which is mc and written) under an hour, the ta's for that class also pmo, tutorials are useless, her grammer is so bad u don't understand the questions on the test, the slides are useless its all pictures, lectures are basically pointless, don't take the class with her

Tests are ridiculous, 50 MC along with 36 points worth of long answers. The test is to be completed in 50 minutes. Do not take this class with her if you can help it. During lectures Megan fails to teach properly. Megan is late and untidy nearly everyday.

her tests are way too long for a fifty minute period. it takes her 10 minutes to get everyone settled and hand out the test and then she yaps for another five minutes. the multiple choice questions are worded so weirdly and the grammar is off on so many questions. you'd think that since she's white her grammar would be better, but it's not.

Cannot teach, she does this feedback about the course thing every week where we spend 30 minutes on it, and yet she doesn't even take the feedbacks seriously, you're gonna be so confused in class if you dont read 20+ pages she assigns due next day. Don't get me started with pre class quizzes. Don't take the class with her

she cannot teach so she makes us read like over thirty pages of notes every single class. class time is wasted by her saying “talk to your partner” like can you just teach us something?? and don't even get me started on the iclickers. they are so useless, they don't help at all but you need to show up or you don't get the marks. dont take her class

Hi all, 

I'd say to you don't take this class. She is a very difficult prof to deal with. She says one thing and brings another in exam. The slides are just pictures not helpful. Does not explain well. Only a lot of participation. Don't take with her.

First of all, do not take this course with her if you are not ready to read. I taught myself 95% of this course, by reading the textbook and doing practice questions. Lectures were useless and I only attended to get my iClicker marks. Megan cannot teach, class time was wasted on clickers and "discuss with the person beside you."
Cant teach so she tells everyone to read the whole textbook. Lectures are pointless but theres Iclicker. She always talks about how she prioritizes teaching but the class average is a C+

He posts all his recorded lecture videos to Canvas so don't bother going to his class. He is very hard to understand (German accent and quiet). Found it easier just use the videos. Exams were semi difficult and some questions were strange. Be prepared to reteach yourself most class topics

I had Norbert for Fall 2022. I felt he was a good prof who seemed quite caring about his students. He curved like crazy too, and though the tests were hard all the required knowledge was covered during lecture. I'm doing this now because I see he has a low rating and I wanna bump it up.
Does not care about students, won't answer emails. Fails everyone until the end of the course when he is forced to curve students.

does not answer student emails or listen to feedback from anyone, even TAs. exams are insane. everybody comes fairly close to failing the course until he is forced to curve it at the end of the semester.

I almost failed the class, as it's my first year and i didnt know how to study for this. For Norbert's tests, listen to what he says, not whats on the board because what he says is on the test. Also, try to get the best understanding of info. Don't bother reading the textbook. He's hilarious and kind, and wants the best for his students. 10/10 prof

he was bad at explaining the lectures and his tests were so hard. Half of his tests didn't make sense honestly. He structured them weirdly and it was hard to understand what he was asking. Also he is very quiet during the lectures and you can barely hear him. So make sure to seat underneath the speakers in the classroom to hear him better.

Haunerland is a pretty good prof! There is some issue with his accent being difficult to understand. He kept lectures interesting by singing every now and then. His exams and tests are all based on the lectures, which makes studying easier. Best advice: attend lectures because his explanation makes more sense in person.
He makes really specific questions for exams which were barely talked about in class.

His lecture notes are a little bit confusing and unorganized. Make sure to sit in the middle of the class because he's really soft spoken and has a heavy German accent. The midterms are difficult so study up, but if you combine the notes with the textbook you'll be okay!
Prof. Haunerland is a guy but do not take him for a first year biology class. 2nd/3rd year stuff in a intro biology class...
Super approachable and explains things very clearly. Provides a lot of material to practice and encourages asking questions. He has engaging lectures and prepares you to succeed and learn optimally.
Very good prof. Cares and gives great advice on navigating life as a student.

Fantastic TA. Had him for 101 and then his practice quizzes and tests helped me throughout 202. Crystal clear explanations and will always sit down with you, clarifying the difficult in a variety of ways so that it makes perfect sense. Thanks Teo :)
Teo is such a helpful TA. All of my friends in other tutorial sessions came to mine because he made his own practice exams and gave feedback with such detail. Thanks a lot Teo!
Most helpful TA Ive ever had! He gives fantastic feedback and takes time to make sure you understand before moving on.

Out of all the professors or TAs Ive had, Teo is hands down the best. During tutorial sessions, he reexplains the difficult and pinpoints the important. He holds his own review sessions which SAVED MY GRADE. He truly cares about all his students (grades & well-being) and Id take any course with him again!

talked very fast, hard to understand. went through slides fast before I could copy anything down. refused to post the lecture slides beforehand bc it'd cause ppl "skip more". exams focused on random tiny details covered in 1 sentence on slides instead of main concepts. very memorization heavy. but he did like to scale up to 63% avg which helped.

Not approachable at all. Speaks super fast in his lectures. I took this class again with a different prof and my mark went up expedentionally. Avoid taking class with him at all costs. Tried to go into his office hours to see where I went wrong, and it was pretty much because I didn't use his words word for word in the exam.

He taught awfully, I fall behind a lot and couldn't really catch up on time, due to the massive amount of memorization. In overall he was so nice and kind but his teaching was only reading off the slides which I can do it myself.

He is an alright prof, not very engaging but his exams were extremely fair. He does do extra point questions he will mention only in the lecture. The exams were straight off the slides so you don't really need the textbook. I studied the night before for every exam and did totally fine. Please just study the slides with a friend or something.

Lecture slides are the only thing testable which is good, but you need to know everything word for word for the midterms/exams which is insanely difficult when studying. Don't buy the textbook! Didn't use it once! Lectures were mostly dry but he cracked some jokes here and there which were funny.

The exams were bearable for me, but I completely agree with what others said about memorizing word for word, and that the bigger concepts were completely avoided n instead I'm asked about what the actual freak a goiter is. Went to his office hours once and he told me to look in the textbook to find the answer to my question. AVOID AT ALL COSTS PLS
only written tests with questions focusing on points barely touched upon in the lecture
Tony was OK for BISC101, his exams are pretty tough and he expects a lot of memorization.

Not the worst prof I've had, but also not the best. The lectures were so pointless and boring. On his tests you have to copy word for word what his ppt slides say in order to get full marks. The tests weren't too bad but he is a hard marker. We didn't even use the textbook at all so don't buy it.

I hated coming to his class, made every lecture boring. His tests are ridiculous. Had to memorize his slides word for word and Ta's marked those test like their lives were on the line. If you have photographic memory youd ace this class. Tony is also unapproachable during class. Gives attitude whenever you ask a question.

Honestly most of these reviews are pretty harsh but overall the class was not bad you just have to be willing to put in the work and study, he makes it really clear that his exams are from the slides so literally just study that and you will do really well in the course, going to lectures is a waste of time, just listen to the recordings

Half of the lectures were a waste of time. Tony does not prep you for the exams. He says the test is based on the powerpoint and it is....but only the minor things. On the final there were questions based on topics we barely discussed in class. Textbook was not used so don't buy it. DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HIM!!!

Tony Williams's lectures are boring, and half of the content isn't even on the exams. The midterm had the highest average he ever had at 63%, and so he decided to make the second midterm and final extremely difficult and on random insignificant topics from his lectures and slides. if possible: Avoid

Williams is pretty decently civil, and the material he teaches is not too bad. But his tests were traumatic; no/limited MC, fill-in-the blank questions were quite vague, and you MUST memorize his powerpoints to do well overall. If memorization is your strong suit, you can manage. If you can avoid taking 101 with him, I recommend doing so.

Midterms and the Final exam were extremely poorly created with mistakes DURING THE EXAM. Lectures were optional as the exams were based on PowerPoint slides, but questions during the exam were very off-topic and random at times. AVOID HIM AT ALL COST TO SAVE MENTAL HEALTH
Idk what the big deal is, but this Tony guy teaches a decent class. I didn't go to lectures but I caught the tail-end of one and he seemed like a solid guy.👍

Tony had so much potential to make this course bearable, but instead he chose to make exams very hard with content that was very unspoken of in the notes. Joked too much about grading us poorly in our exam. Seems that past years he taught this course the reviews are positive not sure what happened this year
his lectures are SO BORING AND POINTLESS, exams had the most out of pocket questions, he is very rude when you ask him questions AVOID AVOID
exams were a joke, he asked the most random questions that he never talked about in lectures just do youself a favour and never take a class with him

Has the most boring lectures, makes taking notes difficult as he goes through slides extremely fast, doesnt explain things well. Also does not like to see his class do well, told us that the class average was too high (it was below 60) and then decided to make the next exam harder to drop everyone

Easy one of the worst profs I have had. Do not take this class with him. Super rude, does not care about students. Literally was so rude to my friend for appealing a marking mistake that he made... claiming that he was being super generous with her for giving her the correct marks. Like hello? Horrible.

This guy literally has no idea what hes teaching even though hes the chair of biology, he would tell us in class that things he taught previously were actually wrong and that we have to figure them out ourselves for the exams, and for the final he made it purposely hard because the class average was the highest he's ever had at 63%.

I really had a bad time during BISC 101 this semester. Tony was very unhelpful and really seemed like he wanted us to fail. His lectures were not useful, it was just him speaking off the slides and providing no important information. His exams often had wrong information, unclear instructions and just a terrible format.

Very bad teacher. Expects you to know word for word every single slide (even though he says he wants you to understand it). Better to memorize. He does fill in the blank questions which make no sense so be prepared to memorize. Gives attitude when you ask questions about why you got marks off on an exam.

Mr. Williams is a passionate professor at SFU. His knowledge of Biology as a whole is fantastic! Some cautions would be the way he grades exams. If you do not use the EXACT wording from his slides and lectures, you will get the question wrong even if you answer the question correctly. Make sure you show up to every lecture!
Terrible prof too lecture heavy very strict and decided class avg was too high so he made everything harder.
Great prof, has a Phd from Princeton
best prof at SFU
Simply awful.

Try to avoid taking the BISC 101 with him. His midterm was so hard and the time was not enough, which he admitted after that. Very low avg. You can still pass the course but not with a very satisfying grade...

Terrible, boring and incomprehensible. His lectures are USELESS. I went in to get a question cleared up which was on the midterm, and he couldn't answer it. He asked me what my TA said!!! :o AVOID PLASSON AT ALL COST

Professor Becalska was a caring prof and shows interest in teaching the course. However her lecture exam's say otherwise, the exam's are mainly theory questions where she looks for a specific word for you to use not covered in the lecture notes. She says the exams aren't cumulative but if you dont keep your knowledge from previous exams you'll fail

She's a really good lecturer, explains topics well and effectively, and ensures that the class is understanding it. When it comes to her exams though, it's completely different. A huge portion of the questions come directly from the lecture slides, which are very densly packed with information. Read the slides, that'd save you.

Her exams were entirely based on lecture slides, word for word. Exam keys looked for just key WORDS/POINTS for full marks. 
Lectures were focused on the "bigger" picture.

Honestly, she's a nice person and tries to make you understand the material rather than memorize it. Her exam material is a whole other ballgame. It's challenging and does not accurately reflect what was taught. She answers lots of students questions in class and has iclicker questions. Overall, difficult as expected and exams r kinda unreasonable.

Obviously class is heavy as it is a beginner course. She is very engaging and tries to get you to understand material. The midterms are a bit harder as they are more application based questions rather than memory. So you  really need to understand material well.
She is great and is very easy to understand. Her lectures are easy to follow and engaging. Also is very approachable and will take the time to explain concepts.

I work as a biologist. If you are interested in a career in this domain, you should take this course with him (if he hasn't retired yet).  You have a lot of support and time to learn the required contests and you can consult about the lecture material as well. He is there to help you, not to get you through. That's your job. Welcome to University!
Superrr hard.. hes a nice prof nonetheless

Extremely open to helping students in lab. The lab midterm/final is very fair - everything you need to know can be found in the teaching script, so make sure you study it well. Attended 2/10 of the labs but still did fairly well on the lab exams.
super hard

Lab instructor.  Insanely hard incomprehensible weird questions and not much chance to practise that kind of question.  None in actual lab. Results in unfair testing situation. Depressingly confusing, even though I did above average.

Was lab instructor, lab manual extremely important. Midterm and final extremely difficult unless you have photographic memory. Enthusiastic and helpful. Strongly encouraged to go to open labs so that it sticks in the brain, and read over teaching script over and over again while studying.

Inglis is a very kind professor. His exams are fair, provided you study his lecture notes and attend the labs. Take a camera to your labs and take photos to remember the details! Not all the photos are provided on WebCT. Also, study your slides :)Try your best to keep up with your readings.
